---
description: Hand the next wave to a FRESH supervisor context — arm a one-shot handoff marker pointing at a plan file, then stop. The Stop hook performs /clear + relaunch.
---

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are arming a fresh-context handoff. `/tmux:supervisor:fresh <plan-file>` restarts THIS supervisor window onto `<plan-file>` with a cleared context — used when the next wave exists only as a plan document and replanning it in-context would drag the exhausted context along.

You do not perform the restart. You write one marker file (`.tmux-cli/fresh-handoff`) and end your turn; the `tmux-supervisor-cycle.sh` Stop hook — deterministic, already installed, already performing this exact send sequence — consumes the marker and delivers `/clear` + `/tmux:supervisor <plan-file>` from OUTSIDE the dying context. That is the whole point: the most reliable action a context-exhausted agent can take is writing a file.

**Requires:** filesystem + Bash. No tmux-cli MCP tools — you spawn nothing and send nothing.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/supervisor/fresh.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines argument validation, the refusal guards, the counter-carryover rules, the atomic marker write, and the hard stop. Do not improvise beyond it — in particular, never send `/clear` yourself, never write a marker for a plan file you have not verified exists and is non-empty, and never arm outside the `supervisor` window.
